generalCitation: Limitations Act, RSA 2000, c L-12, s 3(1)

credit-card.notes: Alberta's 2-year limitation period applies to credit card debts from the date of last activity (payment or written acknowledgement). The 10-year ultimate limitation period also applies — no claim can be brought after 10 years regardless.
credit-card.citation: Limitations Act, RSA 2000, c L-12, s 3(1)

medical.notes: Private medical debts in Alberta follow the 2-year limitation period.
medical.citation: Limitations Act, RSA 2000, c L-12, s 3(1)

auto-loan.notes: Auto loan deficiency balances are subject to Alberta's 2-year limitation period from when the deficiency arises.
auto-loan.citation: Limitations Act, RSA 2000, c L-12, s 3(1)

personal-loan.notes: Personal loans in Alberta are subject to the standard 2-year limitation period.
personal-loan.citation: Limitations Act, RSA 2000, c L-12, s 3(1)

student-loan.notes: Private student loans follow Alberta's 2-year limitation. Government Alberta student loans may have different collection rules.
student-loan.citation: Limitations Act, RSA 2000, c L-12, s 3(1)

mortgage-deficiency.notes: Mortgage deficiency claims in Alberta follow the 2-year limitation period. Note: Alberta's Judicature Act prevents deficiency claims on foreclosure of residential mortgages in many circumstances.
mortgage-deficiency.citation: Limitations Act, RSA 2000, c L-12, s 3(1); Law of Property Act, RSA 2000, c L-7

utility-bill.notes: Utility bill debts are subject to Alberta's standard 2-year limitation period.
utility-bill.citation: Limitations Act, RSA 2000, c L-12, s 3(1)

complaints: File a complaint with Service Alberta at alberta.ca or call 1-877-427-4088. Collection agencies are regulated under the Fair Trading Act, RSA 2000, c F-2, and the Collections Practices Regulation, Alta Reg 194/1999.
